product_name: Mae Pranom Nam Prik Pao (Thai Chili Jam)
notes: Mae Pranom brand already widely available on Amazon with good reviews. One US brand (Thai Home) exists. Would need VERY strong differentiation beyond just rebranding.
Mae Ploy, Aroy-D, Maesri all widely available with strong reviews. Rated #1 by Thai chefs. Single-serve format interesting but low margins on pouches.
Prik Khing is already niche within Thai curry category. Maesri brand available. Keto/low-carb positioning not strong enough twist.
Urban Platter, Raw Essentials, Target Good & Gather all sell palm sugar powder. Shaker bottle format is minor improvement, not transformative.
Many Thai brands widely available. Squeeze bottle format already exists. 'Vinegar alternative' positioning weak.
Red Boat (premium US brand) owns this space. Squid Brand dominant. Fish sauce flight is gimmicky, not solving real problem.

product_name: Thai Instant Tom Yum Soup Cubes
notes: Knorr Tom Yum cubes widely available. Premium paste format exists (many brands). Incremental improvement only.
Por Kwan basil paste already available. Holy basil stir-fry kit competes with many Asian meal kit brands.
Golden Phoenix, Three Ladies, many brands dominate. Single-serve pouches already exist (InnovAsian, Minute Rice microwaveable).
Chaokoh, Aroy-D coconut cream powder widely available. Single-serve sachets exist in Asian grocery stores.
Mama brand already iconic and available in US. Fresh toppings pack for ramen is interesting but logistics nightmare (fresh herbs spoil).
Shrimp paste is polarizing even in mild form. Too pungent for mass US market. Niche product even among Thai food lovers.
Mae Ploy sweet chili sauce ubiquitous. Sriracha brand also makes it. Artisan versions exist. Saturated market.
Healthy Boy, Maekrua, Lee Kum Kee dominate. Vegetarian mushroom oyster sauce already exists (Wan Ja Shan, Kikkoman).
Many Thai brands widely available. Pre-portioned nests minor convenience improvement, not transformative.
Galangal powder exists from multiple brands. Very niche ingredient even among Thai cooks. Low demand.
Lemongrass paste widely available from Asian brands. Squeeze tube format already standard.
Healthy Boy black soy sauce available. Very specific use case (pad see ew). Niche even within Thai cooking.
Sriraja Panich (original Thai brand) available in US. Huy Fong dominates. Fermented artisan sriracha already exists (multiple brands).
